name: Capture Engine
responsibility: Executes the auto-capture pipeline on task switch. Collects environment state (git, files, terminal, window focus, agent states) into an EnvironmentSnapshot. Generates auto resume notes from captured data. Designed to be non-blocking: capture runs asynchronously and must never prevent the user from switching tasks.

interfaces.name: capture_snapshot
interfaces.api_detail: Runs the full capture pipeline asynchronously. Each collector (git, window, terminal, agent) runs independently; if one fails, the others still complete. Returns CAPTURE_PARTIAL if any collector fails. Total pipeline must complete within 2 seconds (INV-003 / REQ-007-AC-4).

interfaces.name: generate_auto_resume_note
interfaces.api_detail: Generates a one-line auto resume note from snapshot data. Template: '{intent_statement} — {active_file}:{line}, agent {status}'. If data is sparse, falls back to intent statement only.

interfaces.name: collect_git_state
interfaces.api_detail: Uses git2 crate. Collects current branch, modified/staged/untracked file counts, and a diff summary (files changed + insertions/deletions). Times out at 1 second.

interfaces.name: collect_window_focus
interfaces.api_detail: macOS: uses Accessibility API (requires one-time permission grant). Windows: uses UI Automation API. Extracts the currently focused application name, window title (often contains file path in IDEs), and attempts to parse file path from title.

interfaces.name: collect_terminal_output
interfaces.api_detail: Reads last 500 characters from the active terminal session's scrollback. Implementation is platform-specific: reads from shell history file or pty output buffer. May return null if terminal is not accessible.

interfaces.name: capture.completed
interfaces.api_detail: Emitted when capture pipeline finishes. Frontend listens to show the optional capture bar (REQ-007-AC-2).

data_models.name: CaptureCollector

fields.name: trait methods
fields.constraints: Each collector implements this trait. Runs with individual timeout (1s per collector).

data_models.note: Collectors: GitCollector, WindowFocusCollector, TerminalCollector, AgentStateCollector. All run concurrently via tokio::join!.

nfr_constraints.performance: Total capture pipeline < 2 seconds. Individual collector timeout: 1 second. Pipeline runs on a dedicated Tokio task, never on the main thread.
nfr_constraints.security: Terminal output is truncated to 500 chars and sanitized (strip ANSI escape codes). Window titles may contain file paths but not file contents. No code content is captured.
nfr_constraints.other: macOS requires Accessibility permission (System Preferences > Privacy & Security > Accessibility). App Shell handles the permission request flow on first launch.
